personas.name: MCST Committee Chair (Premium / EC condo)
personas.icp: Volunteer chair or treasurer of an MCST council at a 200-700-unit private condominium or EC (Park Infinia, Hillsta, Hillington Green, Northwave EC, La Casa EC, Sims Urban Oasis class). Procures security on a 2-3-year cycle through a committee renewal vote. Faces PWM 2024-2028 wage trajectory and a residents' AGM where 'why the bill went up' is question #1.
pains: Renewal vote at AGM is the single accountability moment — needs a printable 'sub-PWM agency vs. PWM-compliant agency' comparison sheet to defend the choice
PWM 2024-2028 wage escalation lifts manpower-only contracts ~6% YoY; residents conflate 'price went up' with 'agency is greedy' even when PWM-driven
Officer attrition / chronic AWOL / no-show on night shift — driven by sub-PWM peers' double-shift abuse — is the #2 complaint-letter driver to the council
Cannot evaluate AI-CCTV / LPR / VMS / command-centre claims technically; needs reference-site walk-through and a costed 'before / after' scenario she can show the AGM
SSIA / PWM Mark / SACE 2026 readiness is now a procurement disclosure requirement under the BCA-Green-Mark / Smart Building tracks her property manager pushes
personas.current_workaround: 2-3-year contract with a mid-tier or sub-PWM agency (Crystal Clear, Ramky, KH Security, Vigilance class) selected by the property manager on lowest-quote basis. Patches with consumer-CCTV (Reolink, Hikvision DIY) and quarterly walk-through audits she runs herself. Forwards complaint letters to the agency manager and hopes he answers.

nba.summary: Sub-PWM mid-tier agency at S$3,200/officer/mo for 1.5 officers + DIY consumer CCTV — works until the AGM challenge or the SACE 2026 audit. Upgrade path is PWM-compliant mid-tier at S$3,400-3,800/officer/mo loaded.

personas.name: Commercial / mixed-use Property Manager
personas.icp: Day-to-day property manager at a Premier Property Consultancy / Smart Property / JLL / Cushman & Wakefield / Knight Frank-managed commercial or mixed-use building (Junction Nine, Seng Kee Building, Fu Lu Shou-area class) acting on behalf of landlord or REIT. Owns the 5-10 site book; reports monthly to the asset manager. Procures on a 2-3-year cycle but renews annually under SLA.
pains: Asset manager / REIT requires monthly incident reporting + annual audit pack + SLA evidence; sub-mid-tier agencies miss this 30% of the time
Landlord wants 'one bill, one vendor' for security manpower + CCTV + LPR + VMS + command-centre; juggling 4-5 vendors burns ops time
PWM 2024-2028 escalator clauses written into leases need a vendor that can supply the PWM-compliance disclosure quarterly without prompting
OBC-style outcome metrics (incident rate, response time, manpower-reduction-via-tech) increasingly written into REIT RFPs; legacy headcount-pricing agencies cannot bid honestly
Tenants escalate via the building chat group within minutes; need a real 24/7 manned command centre that picks up, not a subcontracted overnight monitoring line
personas.current_workaround: Mid-tier security agency for manpower (Concorde / Soverus / Metropolis class) + separate BCA-licensed tech installer (Ademco / Johnson Controls class) for CCTV + separate VMS SaaS subscription + alarm monitoring contract. Manages 4-5 vendors via Excel + email; rebuilds the AR vendor-spend table every quarter.

nba.summary: Multi-vendor stack: 2 PWM-compliant officers + tech installer maintenance + VMS SaaS + alarm monitoring + property-manager admin overhead = ~S$9,500/mo blended for a 1-building scope. Bundle replaces all of it.

personas.name: MNC Plant / EHS Director (industrial)
personas.icp: Plant Director or EHS lead at a SG-based MNC pharma / semicon / FMCG / data-centre operator (Mead Johnson, GlobalFoundries, Micron, Seagate, P&G, Danone class). Budget pre-approved by corporate procurement against a regional spend ceiling. Cares about audit pack + escalation matrix + PWM compliance + business-continuity SLA more than per-officer rate.
pains: Corporate audit cycle requires a complete vendor-readiness binder (bizSAFE Star, ISO 45001:2018, PWM+ Mark, TAFEP, NS Mark Gold, training records, SOPs) within 5 working days of any audit request — sub-tier-1 agencies miss this 50% of the time
Plant volume swings (semicon downturn, pharma capacity bring-up) require demobilise / re-mobilise of 4-12 officers within 2-4 weeks; legacy agencies carry only 10-15% bench depth
Cross-trained officer skills (S5S/GEM/service excellence WSQ + tech-augmented patrol + emergency response + first-aid) are scoring criteria in the parent-MNC global procurement framework
OBC-style outcome metrics (incident rate < 2/site/qtr, response time <90s, false-alarm rate <5%) written into the master service agreement; need vendor that can quantify and report
Officer welfare / TAFEP fair-employment certification is now a global parent-MNC ESG disclosure item — sub-PWM tier creates parent-MNC reputational risk
personas.current_workaround: Tier-1 incumbent (Certis enterprise-account team or AETOS industrial division) at premium APO-inflected rate card S$4,500-5,500/officer/mo loaded, OR a regional MNC security firm (Allied Universal SG ex-G4S, Securitas SG, Prosegur SG) at similar premium. Audit binder is delivered but procurement frame is 'paying premium for the incumbent's binder, not for outcome'.

nba.summary: Tier-1 incumbent (Certis enterprise / AETOS industrial / Securitas SG / Allied Universal SG ex-G4S) at premium rate card: 5 officers × S$4,500/mo loaded + tech installer maintenance + audit-pack admin = ~S$24,500/mo blended for a single MNC plant.

personas.name: Religious site Trustee (church / temple / mosque)
personas.icp: Lay trustee or operations lead at a faith-based site (St Joseph's Church Bukit Timah class — Catholic parish, Buddhist temple, mosque, community hall) reporting to a parish council / temple committee / community board. Hard budget ceiling set annually; high duty-of-care; cost increase only acceptable if backed by a clear net manpower-cost reduction.
pains: Parish council / temple committee approves budget annually; mid-cycle cost increases trigger a special-meeting hassle no trustee wants
Two officers on weekend services + Auto Gate + LPR + CCTV monitoring is the typical scope; legacy agencies bid 2.5-3 officers because 'we always have'
Tech-augmentation (Auto Gate, LPR, Remote Command, virtual patrol) is the only acceptable cost-reduction path — pure manpower cuts read as duty-of-care failure
Discreet uniform + respectful interaction with congregation matters; budget-tier agencies' high-rotation, untrained officers create awkwardness during services
PSG / EDG grant arithmetic is alien to most trustees — they need a vendor who applies on their behalf and reduces the line item directly
personas.current_workaround: Mid-tier or budget agency (KH Security, Vigilance, Magnum Force, Uniforce class) at S$3,000-3,400/officer/mo for 2-2.5 officers, plus an outdated CCTV system installed by a tech vendor 5+ years ago that nobody monitors. Trustee runs the renewal himself by walking the site and asking 'is this still working'.

nba.summary: PWM-floor displaced manpower: 2 officers × S$3,400/mo loaded + ageing-CCTV maintenance amortised + zero command-centre = ~S$7,500/mo. Net to trustee post tech-replacement: ~S$3,300/mo savings.

personas.name: Eldercare Operator (nursing home / hospice)
personas.icp: Operations lead at a nursing home / hospice / day-care centre (Ju Eng Home class — voluntary welfare org or private-equity-backed eldercare operator). Squeezed by MOH per-resident-day funding floors; cannot afford a safeguarding incident (board removal risk); cannot afford 2x night-shift officers either.
pains: MOH per-resident-day rates have not kept pace with PWM lift; security spend faces ~10-15% reduction pressure 2026-27 just to balance the budget
Safeguarding incident on night shift = MOH inquiry + board removal risk + media exposure; cannot run with 1 underqualified officer alone
Night-shift attrition at sub-PWM agencies routinely leaves the home with no officer 2-3 nights/month; relief comes 4 hours late
Tech-augmentation (virtual patrol + facial recognition + intercom + remote command) is the only path that holds duty-of-care while reducing manpower line item
MOH audit pack (bizSAFE Star, ISO 45001, PWM Mark, TAFEP, training records, complaint handling history) is becoming a recurring annual disclosure
personas.current_workaround: Sub-PWM or mid-tier agency at S$3,000-3,400/officer/mo for 2 officers (1 day, 1 night) + basic CCTV + manual visitor logbook. Operations lead patches night-shift gaps personally when relief doesn't show; runs MOH audit pack rebuild from scratch each year.

nba.summary: Pre-tech-augmentation: 2 officers (1D + 1N) × S$3,400/mo loaded = S$6,800/mo. Replace 1 night officer with virtual patrol + facial recognition + intercom @ S$1,200-1,500/mo subscription → net S$5,600/mo + improved coverage.

pricing_models.name: Per-officer-month + tech bundle attach (the Elitez default)
pricing_models.rationale: Aligns with how MCST committees, property managers, MNC plant procurement, religious trustees and eldercare ops actually buy security: a per-officer-month line for manpower (PWM-compliant loaded cost) + a separable tech bundle (CCTV + LPR + VMS + command-centre subscription) priced per-site or per-camera. Maps cleanly to PSG (tech bundle) + EDG (consulting wrap) + WDG(JR+) (workforce-redesign training) grant arithmetic. Lets the buyer scale officers up/down on plant volume swings (MNC) or absorb the PWM lift via tech-attach (MCST, religious, eldercare).

pricing_models.name: Pure per-officer-month (no tech bundle) — the budget-agency default
pricing_models.rationale: Standard sub-PWM / mid-tier pattern. Loses the margin lever (tech bundle yields 30-45% gross vs. manpower 10-15%), penalises the buyer who would rather absorb the PWM lift via tech-augmentation than pay 6%+ YoY on pure headcount. Workable only as a starter SKU for hard-budget religious / eldercare entry tier.

pricing_models.name: Outcome-Based Contracting (OBC) — variable fee on incident metrics
pricing_models.rationale: MHA Security ITM 2025 + REIT procurement (CapitaLand, Mapletree, Frasers) explicitly favour OBC structuring. ~20-30% of fee variable on documented incident rate, response time, manpower-reduction-via-tech. High alignment with sophisticated buyers (REIT, MNC plant); too complex for trustee / nursing-home buyers and risky for the agency without baseline data. Best as a tier variant for commercial + industrial SKU.

pricing_models.name: Site-bundle flat fee (single all-in monthly)
pricing_models.rationale: All-inclusive single line item — manpower + tech + admin. Easiest for the buyer to defend at AGM / parish council, hardest for the agency to manage as plant volume swings. Ideal for MCST + religious + eldercare where scope is stable; risky for MNC plant + commercial where headcount fluctuates.

pricing_models.name: Portfolio rate-card (REIT / property-management firm-wide)
pricing_models.rationale: Single contract across 3-8 buildings managed by the same property-management firm or REIT operator. Reduces per-site procurement overhead; locks in 3-5-year vendor-of-record relationship with REIT. Strategic move for Year-2 onwards once Elitez has 5-10 commercial reference sites.

elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Renewal-vote anchored: BoardRoom Property-Mgmt Pulse 2024 (n=120 SG MCST chairs) flagged 'committee renewal-vote anxiety' as #1 selection factor; 71% would not approve >12% YoY rate hike on like-for-like manpower. PWM 2024-2028 lift forces ~6% YoY but tech-bundle attach can absorb the rest. Walks at >S$7.5k/mo for the typical 1.5-officer + light tech scope. Hard floor at PWM-compliant ~S$4.5k/mo (sub-PWM agencies are non-starters once the renewal questions about SACE 2026 land). Reference reels (Hillington Green SSIA 2023, Park Infinia, Hillsta) reduce elasticity by ~15% — defensible at the AGM.

Multi-vendor consolidation drives 10-15% premium tolerance: AsiaIQ Property-Mgmt Survey SG 2025 (n=180) showed 73% rate 'one-bill admin' as top-3 procurement criterion, beating cheapest-officer-rate (43%). Bundles at S$10-15k/mo against a multi-vendor stack baseline of ~S$9.5k/mo are defensible. OBC-structured pricing for REIT-managed buildings unlocks higher fee with variable component. Walks above S$18k/mo for single-building scope (the budget shifts to portfolio rate-card model).

Audit-pack-driven, not price-driven: corporate procurement budget pre-approved against regional ceiling. AmCham SG Manufacturing 2025 panel: 'we'd rather pay 15% more than face the audit-binder hassle of switching vendors'. Tier-1 incumbents (Certis enterprise, AETOS industrial, Securitas SG, Allied Universal SG ex-G4S) defend the segment at premium APO-inflected rate cards (S$4,500-5,500/officer/mo loaded). Elitez can attack with the same credential stack at PWM-compliant mid-market+ pricing (S$3,800-4,200/officer/mo loaded) — same audit binder, 15-25% lower spend.

Hard budget ceiling set by parish council / temple committee annually. RCEC SG 2025 ('Religious Communities Engagement Council'): 67% of trustees flagged security-spend pressure as 2026 priority. Will only sign IF math is net negative — Auto Gate + LPR + Remote Command @ S$4-5k/mo displacing S$7-9k/mo manpower (St Joseph's Church proof: S$4.2k/mo savings). Walks at >S$8.5k/mo for typical 2-2.5 officer scope without tech-replacement story. PSG eligibility on tech bundle is structurally important — drops effective bundle price ~50%.

MOH per-resident-day funding floors not keeping pace with PWM lift. Tech-augmentation (virtual patrol + facial recognition + intercom replacing 1 night officer at S$1,200/mo savings — Ju Eng Home proof) is the only path. Walks at >S$6.5k/mo for typical 2-officer (1D+1N) scope without tech-replacement. MOH audit pack delivery is the qualification gate — non-compliant agencies are screened out before price comparison. Welfare disclosures (TAFEP, NTUC USE) increasingly part of MOH operator licensing review.

recommended_tiers.name: Estate Watch
what_in: 1.5 PWM+-compliant officers/site, day + evening + weekend roster (loaded cost protected; no double-shift, NTUC-backed welfare)
AI-CCTV monitoring for up to 16 cameras with line-crossing analytics + LPR for 1 vehicle gate
24/7 manned Live Remote Command Centre at CT Hub (virtual guard house — replaces 1 night-officer)
Monthly committee-friendly incident report + annual AGM-ready security review pack
Quarterly site walk-through with property manager + committee chair (joint risk audit)
SSIA / PWM+ / SACE 2026 vendor-disclosure pack (handover-ready for committee)
PSG-eligible tech bundle (single application via BGP)
what_excluded: Auxiliary police / armed officer mandate (not within Elitez PLRD scope)
Custom uniform branding beyond Elitez standard (upgrade to Sentinel)
OBC-structured variable-fee component (upgrade to Sentinel for REIT-managed sites)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$5.7k/mo is what a PWM-compliant agency costs WITHOUT the SSIA proof — and tech-bundle PSG nets you S$4.9k/mo all-in. That's the same spend you have today, with a Live Command Centre attached.

recommended_tiers.name: Sentinel Bundle
what_in: Everything in Estate Watch, plus:
2-3 PWM+-compliant officers (concierge + patrol roster), 24/7 site coverage with backup bench from Adept Academy pipeline
Full integrated bundle: AI-CCTV (32+ cameras) + LPR (multi-gate) + VMS (Visitor Management System with PDPA-ready audit log) + AlarmNet response
REIT-grade monthly incident report + quarterly trend analysis + landlord-ready audit pack (5 working-day SLA)
Outcome-Based Contracting (OBC) variant available: 70% fixed + 30% variable on incident rate / response time / false-alarm rate
BCA-licensed tech installer maintenance + annual hardware refresh allocation
WSQ-trained officers (5S/GEM/service excellence) + tech-augmented patrol routes
EDG-eligible consulting wrap (Human Capital Development pillar) layered onto site engagement
Single-vendor admin: one contract, one bill, one PSG application
what_excluded: MNC plant audit-pack 5-day-SLA across multi-site network (upgrade to Bastion)
Demobilise / re-mobilise on plant volume swings (upgrade to Bastion)
Cross-trained officer skill matrix per parent-MNC global procurement framework (upgrade to Bastion)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$11k/mo replaces a multi-vendor stack of ~S$9.5k/mo plus 4 hours/week of property-manager admin time — net break-even on the line item, plus the property manager gets one bill instead of five.

recommended_tiers.name: Bastion (MNC Plant / Industrial)
what_in: Everything in Sentinel, plus:
5+ PWM+-compliant officers per plant, multi-shift, with 15-25% bench depth from Adept Academy + USE pipeline
Demobilise / re-mobilise SLA: 4-12 officers within 2-4 weeks on plant volume swings
Full MNC vendor-readiness audit binder (bizSAFE Star, ISO 45001:2018, PWM+, TAFEP, NS Mark Gold, training records, escalation matrix, complaint history) — 5-working-day SLA
OBC-structured pricing: 60% fixed + 40% variable on incident metrics + manpower-reduction-via-tech KPIs
Dedicated named account director + 24/7 escalation line direct to CT Hub command floor
Cross-trained officer skill matrix mapped to parent-MNC global procurement framework
WDG(JR+) workforce-redesign engagement (70% rebate, S$50k cap) — Elitez Group as named JR+ consultant on-register
Annual joint plant-tour + risk-assessment workshop with EHS director + parent-MNC corporate security
Quarterly TAFEP / USE-audit-ready welfare pack for parent-MNC ESG disclosure
what_excluded: Auxiliary police / armed mandate (Certis CISCO scope; refer-out partnership available)
Cash-in-transit (Certis / Brink's scope; refer-out partnership available)
M&A / pre-IPO security DD pack (upsell at deal time)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$26k/mo is 15-25% below tier-1 incumbent rate cards (Certis enterprise / AETOS industrial / Securitas SG / Allied Universal SG) for the same SSIA + PWM+ + ISO + bizSAFE + NS Mark + TAFEP credential stack — and EDG + WDG(JR+) drop your effective price below S$22k.

grants.name: PSG (Productivity Solutions Grant) — Security category
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding, ≤200 employees AND ≤S$100M annual revenue. Vendor must be IMDA pre-approved for the Security solutions category (AI-CCTV, VMS, LPR, command-centre). Elitez tech bundle should pursue / maintain pre-approval; manpower component is NOT PSG-eligible.

grants.name: EDG (Enterprise Development Grant) — Human Capital Development pillar
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding, no headcount cap; project must be submitted via BGP before start under Human Capital Development pillar with measurable outcomes; SMEs in Tier-1 priority sectors get 70% support. Applies to consulting wrap (security workforce-redesign assessment, tech-augmentation roadmap), not the recurring manpower or SaaS subscription itself.

grants.name: WDG(JR+) — Workforce Development Grant Job Redesign Plus
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding; engagement must be delivered by a WSG pre-approved JR+ consultant (Elitez Group is on the approved list, LOA valid through 17 Mar 2027) and structured as a workforce-redesign project including training of in-house operations team to operate the post-redesign tech-augmented model.

grants.name: SFEC (SkillsFuture Enterprise Credit)
grants.eligibility: SG enterprise with ≥S$750 SDL contributions over qualifying period AND ≥3 SG local employees; stackable on top of PSG/EDG for the training component of an engagement only, not the SaaS subscription itself.
